mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Tdi engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Tdi engine typically costs £2,500–£4,500 supply only depending on which era (200Tdi or 300Tdi), with used units available from £1,000–£2,200. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on the workshop. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Range Rover.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover Tdi engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — both the 200Tdi and 300Tdi are considered among the most reliable Land Rover engines ever produced. They feature purely mechanical injection systems with no ECU to fail, and examples regularly exceed 250,000 miles with basic maintenance. The 300Tdi is the more refined and widely regarded as the ultimate mechanical diesel.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Range Rover Tdi?

acceptedAnswer.text: On the 200Tdi: injection pump wear causing hard starting and black smoke. On the 300Tdi: timing belt wear (replace every 60,000–72,000 miles). Both engines can suffer fuel lift pump failure. All are well-understood and straightforward to repair by any Land Rover specialist.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Tdi engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and chassis condition. Against a cherished, rust-free Classic Range Rover worth £10,000+, a £3,500 recon is just 35% — well worth it. Against a tired example with significant chassis corrosion, the 60% Land Rover threshold suggests getting a scrap valuation first. Classic Range Rover values are rising, making engine replacement increasingly viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover Tdi engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 200Tdi engine code is 11L (early) or 12L (later) — both are referred to as 200Tdi. The 300Tdi engine code is simply '300Tdi'. Production years: 200Tdi from 1989–1994; 300Tdi from 1994–1996 (UK market) with export and military production continuing to 1998.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 300Tdi timing belt, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: It needs replacement — never attempt to repair a worn timing belt. A preventative replacement every 60,000–72,000 miles (£400–£800) is far cheaper than a snapped belt requiring a full engine rebuild or replacement (£2,800+). On the 200Tdi, the same advice applies — the belt interval is similar.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover Tdi engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare Tdi engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: 200Tdi — Injection Pump Wear
problems.symptoms: Hard starting, rough idle, black smoke, loss of power, poor fuel economy
problems.replacementCost: £2,500–£4,500 (if pump failure leads to engine damage)
problems.recommendation: The Bosch injection pump on the 200Tdi is durable but can wear over time, particularly if contaminated fuel has been used. Refurbishment is a specialist job but well-understood by Land Rover diesel specialists. Don't ignore hard starting or black smoke — these are early warning signs. If the pump fails completely, it can send debris through the injection system.

failureLink.label: Read the full injection pump failure guide

problems.title: 300Tdi — Timing Belt Wear
problems.symptoms: Ticking from front of engine, rough running, sudden loss of power, engine stops

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if belt fails, the engine stops instantly and internal damage is highly likely

problems.recommendation: On the 300Tdi, timing belt replacement every 60,000–72,000 miles is the single most critical maintenance item. Many specialists advise 60,000 miles for peace of mind. A snapped belt means a full engine rebuild or replacement. On the 200Tdi, timing belt intervals are similar but the engine is slightly more forgiving if it fails (though not much). Always demand proof of timing belt history when buying.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing belt failure guide

problems.title: Fuel Lift Pump Failure (Both Engines)
problems.symptoms: Hard starting, lack of power, engine stalling, especially on inclines
problems.recommendation: The mechanical fuel lift pump on both Tdi engines can fail with age. Symptoms include hard starting and fuel starvation at higher speeds or on inclines. Replacement is simple and cheap — it's a DIY-friendly job on these engines. Carry a spare if you're doing serious off-roading.

failureLink.label: Read the full fuel pump failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Tdi — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Viable on a cherished example; borderline on high-mileage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Tdi? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing belt history on 300Tdi (within 60k miles or 5 years)
* No signs of hard starting or black smoke on 200Tdi (injection pump health)
* Full service history with regular oil and filter changes
* A well-maintained example with honest mileage — both engines are known to exceed 250,000+ miles
avoidIf: * 300Tdi with no proof of timing belt replacement — this is the single biggest risk
* 200Tdi with hard starting or excessive black smoke — injection pump work is coming
* Poor service history — these engines are forgiving but not indestructible
* Any signs of chassis corrosion that would make engine replacement uneconomical

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Tdi engines are the definitive mechanical diesels — no ECU, no DPF, no EGR complications. The 200Tdi is slightly more agricultural but exceptionally robust, while the 300Tdi is refined and widely considered the most reliable Land Rover engine ever. Both reward good maintenance with exceptional longevity. The timing belt on the 300Tdi is the one non-negotiable item; everything else is simple, mechanical, and well-understood by UK Land Rover specialists. Buy on history, maintain on schedule, and either engine will outlast the chassis.

cta.label: → Compare Tdi engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Tdi (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Timing Belt History (300Tdi) — Has it been changed within the last 60,000 miles? Any receipt?
2. Cold Start (200Tdi) — Any hard starting or excessive smoke? (injection pump warning)
3. Service History — including which engine era (200Tdi or 300Tdi) and regular oil changes
4. Fuel System Health — Any fuel starvation on inclines? (lift pump warning)
5. Oil Condition — Black is normal; "mayo" under the cap means head gasket trouble
6. Coolant Condition — Any signs of oil in the coolant or coolant loss?
7. Engine Warning Light — There isn't one — that's the beauty of mechanical injection
8. MOT History — Check for corrosion-related failures (chassis, not engine)

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly — two engines across the Classic Range Rover's Tdi production run.
